note: Vetted source pool. Every URL is real and reachable; each `claim` has been corrected to match what the page ACTUALLY says (the loop's verify phase re-confirms live, and any source that fails is gated out before generation). The generator may ONLY cite from this list, and only for the specific claim each source supports.

sources.name: Pharmacy Times
sources.claim: Transdermal patches deliver actives through the skin and avoid the first-pass metabolism that oral pills go through, which can improve how much of the active reaches the bloodstream.

sources.name: Alcohol and Drug Foundation
sources.claim: Heavy alcohol use can inflame the digestive tract and block the absorption of vitamins, and is associated with thiamine (vitamin B1) deficiency.

sources.name: Nature (Scientific Reports)
sources.claim: Hangover severity varies between individuals, with some people more prone to hangovers than others even at smaller amounts of alcohol, and is associated with the amount consumed.

sources.name: Cedars-Sinai
sources.claim: Hangover symptoms are linked to an inflammatory (cytokine) response rather than dehydration, which the article says there is no good evidence for as the primary cause.

sources.name: National Library of Medicine (PMC)
sources.claim: Alcohol hangover is driven by multiple mechanisms and mediators, including inflammation, rather than any single cause.

sources.name: Nutrients (MDPI)
sources.claim: Glutathione (GSH) has been studied for its effects on alcohol metabolism and hangover in a randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial.
